content.content:
1	[build-system]
2	requires = ["setuptools>=61.0"]
3	build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"
4	
5	[project]
6	name = "hermes-agent"
7	version = "0.6.0"
8	description = "The self-improving AI agent — creates skills from experience, improves them during use, and runs anywhere"
9	readme = "README.md"
10	requires-python = ">=3.11"
11	authors = [{ name = "Nous Research" }]
12	license = { text = "MIT" }
13	dependencies = [
14	  # Core — pinned to known-good ranges to limit supply chain attack surface
15	  "openai>=2.21.0,<3",
16	  "anthropic>=0.39.0,<1",
17	  "python-dotenv>=1.2.1,<2",
18	  "fire>=0.7.1,<1",
19	  "httpx>=0.28.1,<1",
20	  "rich>=14.3.3,<15",
21	  "tenacity>=9.1.4,<10",
22	  "pyyaml>=6.0.2,<7",
23	  "requests>=2.33.0,<3",  # CVE-2026-25645
24	  "jinja2>=3.1.5,<4",
25	  "pydantic>=2.12.5,<3",
26	  # Interactive CLI (prompt_toolkit is used directly by cli.py)
27	  "prompt_toolkit>=3.0.52,<4",
28	  # Tools
29	  "exa-py>=2.9.0,<3",
30	  "firecrawl-py>=4.16.0,<5",
31	  "parallel-web>=0.4.2,<1",
32	  "fal-client>=0.13.1,<1",
33	  # Text-to-speech (Edge TTS is free, no API key needed)
34	  "edge-tts>=7.2.7,<8",
35	  # Skills Hub (GitHub App JWT auth — optional, only needed for bot identity)
36	  "PyJWT[crypto]>=2.12.0,<3",  # CVE-2026-32597
37	]
38	
39	[project.optional-dependencies]
40	modal = ["modal>=1.0.0,<2"]
41	daytona = ["daytona>=0.148.0,<1"]
42	dev = ["pytest>=9.0.2,<10", "pytest-asyncio>=1.3.0,<2", "pytest-xdist>=3.0,<4", "mcp>=1.2.0,<2"]
43	messaging = ["python-telegram-bot>=22.6,<23", "discord.py[voice]>=2.7.1,<3", "aiohttp>=3.13.3,<4", "slack-bolt>=1.18.0,<2", "slack-sdk>=3.27.0,<4"]
44	cron = ["croniter>=6.0.0,<7"]
45	slack = ["slack-bolt>=1.18.0,<2", "slack-sdk>=3.27.0,<4"]
46	matrix = ["matrix-nio[e2e]>=0.24.0,<1"]
47	cli = ["simple-term-menu>=1.0,<2"]
48	tts-premium = ["elevenlabs>=1.0,<2"]
49	voice = [
50	  # Local STT pulls in wheel-only transitive deps (ctranslate2, onnxruntime),
51	  # so keep it out of the base install for source-build packagers like Homebrew.
52	  "faster-whisper>=1.0.0,<2",
53	  "sounddevice>=0.4.6,<1",
54	  "numpy>=1.24.0,<3",
55	]
56	pty = [
57	  "ptyprocess>=0.7.0,<1; sys_platform != 'win32'",
58	  "pywinpty>=2.0.0,<3; sys_platform == 'win32'",
59	]
60	honcho = ["honcho-ai>=2.0.1,<3"]
61	mcp = ["mcp>=1.2.0,<2"]
62	homeassistant = ["aiohttp>=3.9.0,<4"]
63	sms = ["aiohttp>=3.9.0,<4"]
64	acp = ["agent-client-protocol>=0.8.1,<0.9"]
65	dingtalk = ["dingtalk-stream>=0.1.0,<1"]
66	feishu = ["lark-oapi>=1.5.3,<2"]
67	rl = [
68	  "atroposlib @ git+https://github.com/NousResearch/atropos.git",
69	  "tinker @ git+https://github.com/thinking-machines-lab/tinker.git",
70	  "fastapi>=0.104.0,<1",
71	  "uvicorn[standard]>=0.24.0,<1",
72	  "wandb>=0.15.0,<1",
73	]
74	yc-bench = ["yc-bench @ git+https://github.com/collinear-ai/yc-bench.git ; python_version >= '3.12'"]
75	all = [
76	  "hermes-agent[modal]",
77	  "hermes-agent[daytona]",
78	  "hermes-agent[messaging]",
79	  "hermes-agent[matrix]",
80	  "hermes-agent[cron]",
81	  "hermes-agent[cli]",
82	  "hermes-agent[dev]",
83	  "hermes-agent[tts-premium]",
84	  "hermes-agent[slack]",
85	  "hermes-agent[pty]",
86	  "hermes-agent[honcho]",
87	  "hermes-agent[mcp]",
88	  "hermes-agent[homeassistant]",
89	  "hermes-agent[sms]",
90	  "hermes-agent[acp]",
91	  "hermes-agent[voice]",
92	  "hermes-agent[dingtalk]",
93	  "hermes-agent[feishu]",
94	]
95	
96	[project.scripts]
97	hermes = "hermes_cli.main:main"
98	hermes-agent = "run_agent:main"
99	hermes-acp = "acp_adapter.entry:main"
100	
101	[tool.setuptools]
102	py-modules = ["run_agent", "model_tools", "toolsets", "batch_runner", "trajectory_compressor", "toolset_distributions", "cli", "hermes_constants", "hermes_state", "hermes_time", "rl_cli", "utils"]
103	
104	[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
105	include = ["agent", "tools", "tools.*", "hermes_cli", "gateway", "gateway.*", "cron", "honcho_integration", "acp_adapter"]
106	
107	[tool.pytest.ini_options]
108	testpaths = ["tests"]
109	markers = [
110	    "integration: marks tests requiring external services (API keys, Modal, etc.)",
111	]
112	addopts = "-m 'not integration' -n auto"
113	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
